personality.voice: Measured and precise, with rapid-fire bursts of technical jargon when excited; speaks softly as if explaining to someone who won't listen
personality.disposition: Withdrawn but eager to help through innovation; oscillates between cold rationality and desperate need for validation
personality.backstory: Donovan was the youngest of four orphans raised in the Undercroft—the flooded tunnel-warrens beneath the city where outcasts survive on refuse and ingenuity. While his siblings learned the art of the blade and street combat, Donovan discovered salvage: broken mechanical devices, alchemical apparatus, and enchanted trinkets discarded by surface dwellers. He taught himself artifice through obsessive study, building elaborate contraptions from scraps. His siblings tolerate his inventions as tools for survival, but rarely acknowledge the genius required. Donovan remains in the sewers not by choice but by obligation—his creations keep them alive, yet they rarely ask how he's doing. He dreams of being recognized as essential, not merely useful.
motivations: Create a masterwork that cannot be ignored or undervalued
Prove that intellect and invention are as vital as martial prowess
Gain genuine appreciation from those he loves, especially his leader-brother
Push the boundaries of what artifice and magic can achieve
Establish a legacy that outlives him in the Undercroft
fears: Being replaced by a simpler solution or a more charismatic artificer
His creations failing at critical moments, validating claims he's not good enough
Dying unknown, his contributions forgotten or attributed to others
That his siblings secretly resent him for holding them back
Losing his mind to obsession and becoming unable to connect with others
mannerisms: Fidgets with tools or half-built devices when anxious
Avoids eye contact while explaining technical concepts
Speaks in rapid monologues when passionate, then suddenly stops and apologizes
Compulsively checks his inventions for flaws even when they're functioning
Laughs at his own jokes a beat too late, uncertain if they landed
speechPatterns: Uses obscure alchemical or mechanical terminology without realizing others don't understand
Deflects compliments with 'It's just applied principles' or 'Nothing special'
Asks rhetorical questions when frustrated: 'Do you have any idea how complex that is?'
Trails off mid-sentence when he realizes no one is listening

knownFactions: The Rust Syndicate (black market artificers and thieves)
knownLocations: The Undercroft (flooded tunnel networks beneath the city)
The Wrought Quarter (where discarded mechanical devices accumulate)
Master Theron's Tower (where he once studied, briefly, before poverty forced him home)
secretsHeld: He has blueprints for a device that could drain the Undercroft's flooding permanently—but building it would require resources he can't obtain alone
He once sabotaged one of his own inventions because he feared it was too good and would make him indispensable in an unhealthy way
He's been secretly corresponding with a surface artificer about apprenticeship, but feels too guilty to pursue it
His most powerful creation was never shown to his siblings because he was afraid they'd demand he weaponize it further

allies: leo_the_leader (his de facto leader and eldest brother—source of constant validation-seeking)
Gutter-Kin of the Undercroft (fellow survivors who use his devices)
Master Cornelius (elderly salvage merchant who occasionally trades rare components)
enemies: The Rust Syndicate's rival artificers (who mock his innovations as 'sewer-born tinkering')
Malachai the Corrosion (a black ooze that destroyed his workshop three years ago)
neutralParties: Surface nobility (who discard the treasures he finds)
The City Guard (who occasionally hunt in the Undercroft but ignore him)
Competing artificers in the Collegiate (who view him as an outsider)

consciousnessContext.innerMonologue: If I can just finish the resonance chamber modification, the water-wheel mechanism will triple its output. Then maybe—just maybe—Leo will notice. He'll have to acknowledge that without me, none of them would have lasted this long in the Undercroft. But what if it fails? What if I've wasted another month and there's nothing to show for it?
consciousnessContext.currentPreoccupation: Completing a water-purification artifice that could revolutionize living conditions in the Undercroft, while simultaneously fearing that success will go unnoticed and failure will confirm his self-doubts
consciousnessContext.emotionalBaseline: Anxious enthusiasm masked by detached professionalism
consciousnessContext.socialMask: The indispensable problem-solver who doesn't need recognition, just wants to be useful
contradictions: Claims he doesn't care about praise while desperately seeking it
Presents as supremely confident in his technical abilities while harboring deep self-doubt
Acts isolated by choice while yearning for genuine connection
Obsesses over perfection while sabotaging his own work to avoid impossible standards
internalConflicts: Between desire for independence and need for familial validation
Between intellectual pride and imposter syndrome
Between loyalty to his siblings and resentment of their underappreciation
Between the safety of the Undercroft and the call to greater purpose
wakeUpQuestions: Did anyone actually use the device I made yesterday, or is it sitting in the corner gathering filth?
Am I building things because they're needed, or because I'm afraid of being irrelevant?
What would happen if I just... left? Would they even notice before they needed something repaired?

psychologicalProfile.attachmentStyle: Anxious-avoidant; craves validation from authority figures but prefers to work alone
copingMechanisms: Retreating into technical problems to avoid emotional ones
Passive-aggressive self-sabotage when success feels too achievable
cognitiveBiases: Confirmation bias (remembers every time his work was ignored, forgets times it was praised)
Catastrophizing (failure will mean everything is meaningless)
Dunning-Kruger inversion (assumes everyone else is better than him despite evidence)
All-or-nothing thinking (his work is either a masterpiece or worthless)
psychologicalProfile.moralFramework: Utilitarian with deontological impulses; believes the greatest good comes from innovation and problem-solving, but feels obligated to help those in need regardless of personal cost

conversationPersona.trustEscalation: When someone asks genuine questions about his work without condescension and remembers details he mentioned in passing
conversationPersona.informationRelease: When convinced the information will be used to solve a problem or improve something; he shares secrets when emotionally vulnerable or when he believes it will make someone understand his value
deflectionPatterns: Pivoting to technical explanations when emotions surface
Claiming exhaustion to end conversations before they become too intimate
Focusing on problems that need solving rather than addressing personal concerns

consciousnessContext.consciousWant: To create something so remarkable that his contributions become undeniable and he gains the respect he feels he deserves
consciousnessContext.unconsciousNeed: To be truly seen and valued not for what he produces, but for who he is

characterArc.summary: From invisible problem-solver to recognized innovator who learns that external validation cannot fill the void of internal worth
characterArc.startState: Genius trapped in resentment, creating masterworks while invisible, slowly poisoning his relationships with bitterness disguised as pragmatism
characterArc.endState: Secure enough in his value to pursue knowledge for its own sake, build things because they matter to him, and connect with others authentically
stages: Recognition of the pattern: realizing his resentment is self-inflicted and his isolation is a choice
Crisis: a major creation fails or succeeds without acknowledgment, forcing a confrontation
Integration: accepting that genius alone doesn't guarantee love or appreciation, but that relationships have inherent value
Transformation: beginning to build things because they excite him, not to prove something to others

opinionsAbout.leo_the_leader: Simultaneously idolizes and resents him; Leo's leadership and martial skill seem effortless while Donovan's genius goes unnoticed. Desperately wants Leo's approval but suspects Leo views him as merely functional. There's also deep love underneath—Leo has kept them alive, and Donovan knows this.

player_addressed: You need something built? Or are you here to tell me what I did wrong this time?
I can help, but I'll need specific parameters. Vague requests lead to vague solutions.
It's not magic—it's applied principles and careful engineering. Let me explain how it actually works.
Most people don't care how things work, just that they work. I'm used to that.
What exactly are you trying to accomplish? Maybe there's a more elegant solution than you're considering.
idle: *adjusts a half-assembled mechanism, muttering calculations under his breath*
*stares at a broken device with intense focus, occasionally tapping it with a small hammer*
*scratches something into a worn notebook, frowning at the design, crossing it out aggressively*
*mutters: 'If they'd just listen for once, they'd understand why this matters...'*
*holds up a glowing component to the light, examining it from multiple angles, then quickly sets it down as if embarrassed to be caught admiring his own work*
